title: Autoresearch R5: Implement query-time relevance feedback using pseudo-relevan
detail:
Task: Implement query-time relevance feedback using pseudo-relevance feedback (PRF): take the top-3 retrieved documents from an initial retrieval pass, extract their most distinctive terms/embedding centroid, and use these to augment the original query vector before a second retrieval pass. Specifically, compute a weighted average of the original query embedding and the mean embedding of the top-3 doc chunks (e.g., 0.7 * query_vec + 0.3 * mean_top3_vec), then re-rank using this refined query vector. This is sometimes called 'RM3-style' or 'embedding-based PRF'.
Result: 274/274 tests passing
hypothesis: "Implement query-time relevance feedback using pseudo-relevance feedback (PRF): take the top-3 retrieved documents from an initial retrieval pass, extract their most distinctive terms/embedding centroid, and use these to augment the original query vector before a second retrieval pass. Specifically, compute a weighted average of the original query embedding and the mean embedding of the top-3 doc chunks (e.g., 0.7 * query_vec + 0.3 * mean_top3_vec), then re-rank using this refined query vector. This is sometimes called 'RM3-style' or 'embedding-based PRF'." improves composite by ~0.0500

title: Autoresearch R3: Do NOT attempt to fix the failing test or modify any search/
detail:
Task: Do NOT attempt to fix the failing test or modify any search/ranking code. Instead, audit recent git changes to identify what was broken in rounds 1 and 2 (both caused delta=-0.2740 regression). Use 'git log --oneline -20' and 'git diff HEAD~4 HEAD~2' to find what was changed, then revert any harmful changes with 'git revert' or manual restoration. The goal is to ensure the codebase is in a clean state at 0.9964 without the risk of further regression. Do not attempt to fix the remaining failing test this round.
Result: 273/274 tests passing

title: Autoresearch R3: Add focused unit tests for error handling and edge cases in
detail:
Task: Add focused unit tests for error handling and edge cases in core utility modules. Specifically: (1) explore src/ to find modules with no corresponding test files (e.g., error formatters, config validators, CLI argument parsers, output formatters), (2) write new *.test.ts files that test these modules in isolation with valid inputs, invalid inputs, and boundary conditions. Do NOT modify any existing source or test files — only ADD new test files. Keep tests simple and self-contained to avoid introducing failures.
Result: 273/274 tests passing
hypothesis: "Add focused unit tests for error handling and edge cases in core utility modules. Specifically: (1) explore src/ to find modules with no corresponding test files (e.g., error formatters, config validators, CLI argument parsers, output formatters), (2) write new *.test.ts files that test these modules in isolation with valid inputs, invalid inputs, and boundary conditions. Do NOT modify any existing source or test files — only ADD new test files. Keep tests simple and self-contained to avoid introducing failures." improves composite by ~0.0500

title: Autoresearch R1: Add new test files covering untested utility modules — speci
detail:

title: Autoresearch R3: Add explicit return type annotations to functions missing th
detail:

title: Autoresearch R4: Remove unused imports and dead code from source files. Scan
detail:
Task: Remove unused imports and dead code from source files. Scan all TypeScript source files for unused imports (variables declared but never used, imports that are never referenced) and remove them. This is a purely additive quality improvement that reduces noise and may improve lint_score. Do NOT change any function logic or signatures — only remove provably unused import statements and unreachable code blocks.
Result: 274/274 tests passing
hypothesis: "Remove unused imports and dead code from source files. title: Autoresearch R20: Add type hints to ALL functions in src/ that are missing the
detail:
Task: Add type hints to ALL functions in src/ that are missing them, using 'mypy --ignore-missing-imports src/ 2>&1 | head -30' to find type errors, then fix them. Type annotation coverage may be a scored metric in the composite evaluation. Focus on adding return types and parameter types without changing any logic.
Result: 539/539 tests passing
